This company was registered in the Transvaal March 27th, 1895, to acquire from the Randfontein Estates Gold Mining Co., Witwatersrand, Ltd., a block of 311 claims on farms Bandfontein and Uitvalfontein, Western Witwatersrand, Trans vaal. The consideration was ú387,500, in fully-paid shares. The property includes the mine formerly worked by the Bandfontein Estates Co., with which a 60-stamp mill and oyanide works in operation were taken over. Early in 1897 the company sold about half its property to the South Bandfontein Co. for 250,000 shares, of which 100,004 are still held. Tho company s mining area now comprises 170 claims. Crushing was commenced on the property by the Bandfontein Estates Co. in September, 1898, with 40 stamps ; an additional 60 stamps were aftorwards added, making 100 now at work, also three tube mills added in 1908 and cvanide plant. During 1907, 175,466 tons yielded ú315,185 ; profit 110,504 or 12s. 71d. per ton ; dnring 1908 218,850 tons yielded ú343,655 ; estimated profit ú120,024. For crushings see table at commencement of volume. Ore reserves at December 31st, 1907, 327,673 tons value ll-8dwts. per ton and 94,883 tons value 4 5dwts. per ton ; at September 30th, 1908, 603,237 tons. The authorised capital is ú500,000, in 500,000 shares of ú1 each ; all the shares are issued and fully paid. In 1896, 50,000 shares were sold at ú2 each. In 1903, ú150,000 six per cent, debentures were issued, repayable in twelve annual instalments commencing after three years, or at company s option at any time upon giving six months notice ; a further ú100,000 debentures since issued, making ú250,000 outstanding at December, 1907, bearing 5 per cent, interest. Dividends declared paid about two months after declaration : For 1895, 10 per cent. ; for 1897, 10 per cent. ; for 1898, 10 per cent. A distribution of 100,000 South Bandfontein snares was announced in January, 1902, being one for every tire Porges shares held on February 5th, 1902 ; shareholders had the option of taking ú3 cash instead of the share, making the distribution equal to 12s. cash per Porges share ; since then the following have been declared : for 1905, 10 per cent. ; for 1906, 10 per cent.f 1907, 10 percent. ; 1908. 10 per cent. December 21st, 1908. This company was registered in the Transvaal December 1st, 1902, and owns the entire freehold farm Elandsfontein, No. 85, comprising 3,800 acres, situate in the Pretoria district of the Transvaal, with the Premier Diamond Mine thereon. The original vendors received 67,000 fully-paid shares for part of the farm with mine and ú25,650 cash has since been paid for the remainder of the freehold. In 1904, the company acquired the adjoining farm LouwBbaken, No. 399, area 1,931 morgen, and in 1905 a portion of Farm Spitzkop, No. 31, 829 morgen, and portion of farm Znikerboschfontein, No. 325, 1,136 morgen, were acquired. In September, 1908, the property of the Pretoria District Diamond Co., Ltd., comprising 176 morgen adjoining on the west, was acquired for ú21,650. The mine covers an area of abont 3,570 claims j the blue ground has been proved by a borehole to 1,000ft. depth ; plant to treat 24,500 loads per day has been erected. Under Transvaal law, the Government is entitled to six-tenths of tho profits from the mine, after repayment of the company s capital expenditure. The rim-rook so far as can be judged is not likely to give trouble from falls of reef to any appreciable extent,and theopen working might therefore be to say 1,200 or 1,500 feet or more, which will represent approximately 500,000,000 loads. Taking 400,000,000 loads only, this gives you 26 years in the open, or at the present rate of working, say, 50 years. As the mine increases in depth it will be necessary to sink a shaft outside the rimrock, and to drive a tunnel from the shaft to the open mine. This cost will not be neoessary for some time to come, as the present system of working can be to a depth of at least 100 feet, which would represent approximately 136,000,000 loads, or say, 9 years work. Ths capital was altered to present form in January, 1905, each ú1 share being divided into two preference shares of 5s. each and four deferred shares of 2s. 6d. each. The properties include the eastern portion of the freehold farm Witpoort No 162, East Band district, and farms Kameelpoortnek No. 354, Brakfontein No. 249, Gemsbokfontein No. 65, and Vlaklaagte No. 39 on the Bnshveld Tin Fields, Pretoria district. Tin was discovered on the last-named farm and a ton per cent, interest held by the discoverers was purchased in 1905 for 25,000 fully-paid shares ; the company erected a 10-stamp mill and crushing commenced in February, 1906, but was suspended in August, 1906. Prospecting is in progress. A syndicate has acquired the right to prospect the Witpoort property in conjunction with that of the East Rand Central Mines, Ltd., and to form a company with ú1,000,000 capital to work both properties. The authorised capital is ú399,000, in 399,000 shares of ú1 each ; all the shares are issued and fully paid. The capital was increased from ú340,000 to present amount in July, 1905, 34,000 shares being offered to shareholders at ú2 15s. each ; the guarantors of the issue received an option for six months over 26,000 shares at ú3 10s. each, and 25,000 shares were issued in payment for interest in two farms as above. The former Mount Lyell Mining and Railway Co., Ltd, was registered in Melbourne, Victoria, in 1893, being a reconstruction of a former company of same name formed to ac quire copper mining leases comprising 333 ac es situate at Mouut Lyeil, about 18 miles from Macquarie Harbour, on the west coast of Tasmania, also 43 acres for reduction works site. The ore of the mines consists of a mixture of iron and copper pyrites with silver and gold. A special lease for 30 years was granted by the Tasmania Legislature in September, 1893. The company constructed a railway from the mines to Macquarie Harbour. In August, 1900, the adjoining property of the South Tharsis Mining Co., No Liability, was purchased for ú46,000 cash ; the adjoining Boyal Tharsis Mine, area 40 acres, was also acquired at a total cost of ú13,347, and the King Lyell Mine at a cost of ú2,000. The North Mount Lyell Copper Co., Ltd., was registered on 20th September, 1897, to acquire the property of the North Mount Lyeli Mining Co., No Liability, of Melbourne vol. for 1902 , consisting of 30 acres of mining land, in three contiguous blocks of 10 acres each, situate in the northern portion of the Mount Lyell field, Tasmania, and held under mining leases from toe Crown; also the right to construct a railway 28 miles long from the mines to Macquarie Harbour. The railway was constructed, and wharf, pier and jetty at Macquarie Harbour completed. The company also erected smelting works. This company was registered at Rockhampton, Queensland, on the 1st October 1886, to acquire the Mount Morgan Gold Mine, consisting of 640 acres freehold, amd . 93 MOrai MOBGAN GOLD- about 90 acres held under a perpetual lease from the Crown. The property is situate about 25 miles from Rockhampton, Queensland. The consideration was ú875,000, payable by the issue of the whole of t;he shares oredited with 17s. 6d. as paid. In 1895, owing to a question arising as to ownership of gold obtained from freehold land, the directors converted 75 acres of freehold into three leases of 25 acres each. In 1903 the company acquired two mining leases covering 25 acres situate under its water rights, and also took up leases covering 480 acres along Eiver Dee, 10 secure outlet for tailings, Ac, annual rental ú1 per acre. An area of 30 acres containing limestone, 53 miles distance from the mine, has been acquired to provide flax for the copper reduction works, and in 1906 the Many Peaks Copper Mine, containing an extensive lode of pyritio ore was acquired. In 1907 it was decided to erect an electrolytic refinery for treating the blister copper to save shipping same to America for treatment and the Electrolytic Refining and Smelting Co. of Australia, Ltd., has been formed to erect smelting works near Sydney and an agreement entered into with that company to treat the blister copper for a period of ten years. Various reduction works are in operation. A discovery of gold-copper ore occurred in 1903, and a separate plant consisting of four blast furnaces and oonrerters has been erected and smelting operations commenced January, 1906. Tnrs company was first registered in the Transvaal September 8th, 1887, as the Bantjes Reef Gold Mining Co., L td., to acquire 104 gold-miningelaims on the Farms Vogelstruisfontein and Langlaagtc, WitwaterBrand, Transvaal The vendors received ú70,000 in fully-paid shares. In December, 1888, 16 claims on the Langlaagte Farm, with adjoining inynpacht 33 morgen, were sold for ú60,000 cash. In 1893 six south claims were purchased for ú1,150. On September 9th, 1895, the company was reconstructed in the Transvaal under the present title, various adjoin ing properties being acquired for 196,250 shares . A further 25 claims were after wards acquired for 16,700 shares. Early in 1896, the company purchased for ú76,000 cash the freehold of 3,148 acres, comprising about half of the farm Vogel struisfontein known as Van der Hoven s portion, including the part on which the company s mining claims arc situated, also including the Old and New Florida Township and the Florida Lake water-right. Mining area now held, 813 claims, also four water-rights. Three shafts are being sunk, and at September 30th, 1909, the east incline shaft has been sunk to a depth of 1,650 feet, the central shaft to 810 feet and the west shaft to C48 feet ; ore reserves at September 80th, 1909, 580,707 tons, value 6 4dwts. per ton. A 70-stamp battery tube mill, and cyanide and slimes plant are being erected, and crushing is expected to commence about Maroh, 1910. Provided the development operations continue satisfactory the estimated life will exceed 40 years treating 120,000 tons yearly. A revenue is derived from claim licences on the freehold and from stands in the Old Florida Township j the New portion is about to be dealt with. The authorised capital is ú322,123, in 322,123 shares of ú1 each; 306031 shares are issued and fully paid. Under the chome of reconstruction in 1895, five fully-paid new shares were given for every four old shares, with the right to subscribe for one new share at 50s. This company was registered in the Transvaal September 20th, 1902, to acquire the undertakings of the New Crcesns Gold Mining Co., Ltd. registered October, 1891 , and the Langlaagte Star Gold Miniag Co., Ltd. registered 1894 see vol. for 1905 . The property consists of a compact block of 343 claims and 210 deep level claims on the farm Langlaagte, four miles west of Johannesburg, Transvaal, also three pieces of ground equal to 30 claims bold under lease for erecting plant or buildings and six wa er rights. The deep lovel claims were acquired in Novem ber, 1908, from the Johannesburg Consolidated Investment Co., Ltd., for 114,218 fully-paid shares. Owing to the intrusion of the FerreiraCrown Reef dyke, which has caused an upthrow of the reef, two new shafts are being sunk on the southern portion of the property, and when sufficient development work has been done, a 200-stamp mill and tube mills are to be erected. The present mill consists of 140 stamps, also cyanide works. Ore reserves at December 31st, 1908, 504,540 tons, value 8 6dwts. per ton, as against 444,427 tons, value 9dwts. per ton at December, 1907. The company took over 751$ main reef claims and an estate of 7 66 square miles including the Maraisburg township , situate on the farm Paardekraal No. 42, about six miles west of Johannesburg, Transvaal j the com pany has since acquired the freehold of the farm. In May, 1899, two subsidiary companies. Main Reef Deep, Ltd., and Main Reef East, Ltd., were formed bat in June, 1909, these companies were absorbed see below . The company in 1905 acquired from the liquidators of the Rand Central Ore Redaotion Co., Ltd., 18 claims, 66 bewaarplaatsen, 2 machine stands and a water right, for the nominal sum of ú6. London Secretaries and Offices Johannesburg Consolidated Investment Co., Ltd., 10 and 11, Austin Friars, E.C. Phone : UK Wall. This company was registered in the Transvaal November 29th, 1886, to acquire the farm Driefontein, Eastern Witwatersrand, 10,355 acres in extent, with, a mynpacht of 300 claims. The vendors received ú45,000 in cash and ú150,000 in shares. In January, 1889, the company sold 36 claims for ú210.000 in cash and ú15,000 in shares of the Balmoral Main Reef Gold Mining Co., Ltd. In May, 1893, 12 claims adjoining the Gleiicairn property, with the corresponding 48 dip claims, making 60 in all, were Bold to Barnato Bros, for ú60,000. During 1909, 20 claims Bituate to the north were acquired for ú1,500. The company now owns Mynpacht No. 320, 296 morgen in extent, equal to 427 5 claims, 102 prospecting claims, four water rights, and 4,780 morgen freehold on farm Drietfontein, No. 148, Boksbnrg district, Transvaal, and also derives a revenue of about ú11,000 per annum from a residential estate of 4,892 acres, situate on the said farm to the north of the main reef series. Crushing commenced in January, 1888, with a 50-stamp mill, and further 50 Btamps were afterwards added, but crushing was suspended in 1891 and the mine has since been thoroughly developed and a new 120-stamp mill and cyanide plant erected, crushing being resumed in 1896. A further 100 stamps making 220 and slimes plant have been added and are now at work. During 1908 407,990 tons yielded ú522,890 ; net profit ú179,893 or 8s. 9-8d. per ton ; costs 16s. 97d. per ton ; during 1909, 363,620 tons yielded ú429,224 estimated profit ú171,700 operations interfered with due to floods . For crushings see tableat commencement of volume. Ore in sight at December, 1907, 1,094,767 tons ; at December 31st, 1908, 1,110,976 tons. The authorised capital is ú425,000, in 425,000 shares of ú1 each ; all the shares are issued and fully paid. The capital, formerly ú210,000 in ú10 shares. . 675 WITWATEE8KAND GOLD was increased and altered in 1888 to ú250,000 in ú1 shares ; increased in 1896, to ú326,000, 62,500 shares being issued at ú4 ; in 1897, 12,817 shares were sold at ú4 12s. 3d. average ; in 1899, capital was increased to ú376,000, and 27,083 shares issued to shareholders at ú6 ; the remaining 22,917 were issued in 1902 at ú7 10s., the Johannesburg Consolidated Investment Co., Ltd., beiag allotted 3,357 at that price and guaranteeing the remaining 19,560, which were offered to shareholders. In June, 1904, the capital was increased to present amount and 37,600 shares were offered to shareholders at ú5 10s. each, the issue being guaranteed ; the remaining 12,500 were sold at ú5 each in May, 1905. A dividend of 15s. per share was paid March, 1889, oat of cash proceeds of sale of claims ; the 15,000 Balmoral shares were also distributed. Later dividends declared paid about two months after declaration : For 1898, 80 per cent, j 1905, 15 per oent. ; 1906, 25 per cent. ; 1907, 30 per cent. ; 1908, 37J per cent. 17t per oent. Jnne 19th and 20 per oent. December 23rd ; 1909, 35 per cent. 17J per oent. June 21st and 17J per eent. December 17th . The accounts for year ended December 31st, 1907, submitted in Johannesburg March 25th, 1908, showed gold receipts ú539,820 and a proBt of ú176,091, making with balance forward and sundry revenue a sum of ú480,474 ; dividends absorbed ú127,500, depreciation written off ú249,166, taxes ú12,575, leaving ú91,233 carried forward. The accounts to December 31st, 1908, submitted in Johannesburg Maroh 31st, 1908, showed gold receipts ú522,890 and a profit of ú179,893 making wish balance forward and sundry revenue a sum of ú291,866 ; dividends absorbed ú159,375, tax, etc. ú13.116, leaving ú119 375 carried forward. Share premium account ú55,158. A share register is kept in London. Transfer form, common ; fee, 2s. 6d. per transfer per 100 shares or part. The 28th October, 1896, was appointed settling day in shares Nos. 1 to 311,980 ; October 26th, 1899, in Nos. 311,981 to 352,083 ; August 13th, 1902, in Nos. 852,084 to 375,000 ; September 14th. 1904, in Nos. 375,001 to 412,500 ; and August 15tb, 1905, in Nos. 412,501 to 425,000. Phone : 2823 Wall. company was registered in the Transvaal March 29th, 1888, to acquire about 3,341 acres of freehold land and water-rights, situate on the farm Boodepoort No. 43, Western Witwatersrand, Transvaal, including two mynpaohts equal to 290 claims, with machine sites and water rights. In March, 1911, the under takings of the following companies were acquired as from December 31st, 1910, vii. : Boodepoort Central Deep, Ltd., for 108,333 fully-paid shares, West Roode poort Deep, Ltd., for 69,000 fully- paid shares, and Norman Properties Synd., Ltd., for ú14,649 cash. Tho total area now held comprises 680 mining claims lying to the south of the Main Beef outcrop, and at December, 1910, it was estimated the Main Beef remained intact in 665 claims and the South Beef in 621 claims. Several ether companies properties are Bituate on the estate, the Princess Co. receiving licences and rentals. In the year 1892, 30 stamps were working ; a oyanide plant started in 1894. A new 30-stamp mill was started in December, 1895, in place of the old one, and 30 new stamps were subsequently added, making 60. On the acquisition of the undertaking of Roodepoort Contral Deep, Ltd., in March, 1911, a further 50 stamps were acquired, making a total of 110 stamps now at work. A further 10 stamps and five tube mills are to be added. During 1910, 120,414 tons yielded gold value ú195.499; profit ú58,431 or 9s. 8d. per ton excluding sundry revenue . During 1911, 166.368 tons yielded ú248,584; estimated profit inoluding sundry revenue ú26,534. For details of crushings see table of commencement of volume. Ore reserves at December, 1910, 647,000 tons of which 582,000 tons had a value of S ldwts. and 65,000 tons value 6 0dwts. per ton. The authorised capital is ú650,000, in 650,000 shares of ú1 each ; 575,033 shares are issued and fullv paid. The capital was increased in 1889 from ú125,000 to ú150,000 ; reduced in 1890, by reconstruction, to ú80,000; increased in 1894 to ú165,000 ; 80,000 of the new shares being issued at par and 5,000 at ú3 each. During 1908-9, 100,000 shares were taken up by A. Goerz & Co, Ltd., at par. In June, 1904, the directors were authorised to increase the capital to ú325,000. In March, 1911, the capital was increased to present amount to acquire the various undertakings mentioned above, and 132,700 shares were Bnbscribed by A. Thone: 2151 Wall, Tms company was registered in the Transvaal July 18th, 1895, and is a recou strnction of the Vogelstruis Deep, Ltd. The property is situate on the farm Vogelstrnisfontein, Western Witwatersrand. Twenty-five claims were sold in August, 1895, to tho Bantjes Consolidated Mines, Ltd., for 16,700 fully-paid Bantjes shares. The company now owns 346 claims on the dip of the Vogelstruis Estates property, also three water rights. Three shafts have been sunk. From September, 1906, to September, 1908, the mine was closed down. A 40-slamp battery, also cyanide slimes plant are erected and crushing commenced January, 1910, but was suspended Jnly, 1910, owing to further development being required. An issue of debentures was made June, 1910, to provide funds for an extensive scheme of development, but owing to insntficient subscriptions being received all operations were suspended. In June, 1911, negotiations were entered into with the Roodepoort United Main Beef Gold Mining 00., Ltd , with a view to selling the undertaking to that company, but subsequentiy the scheme was abandoned. Payable ore reserves at date of closing down 184,630 tans, value 5 92dwts. over 37 inches South Reef and 14,900 tons, value 5 96dwts. over 70 inches Main Reef. The authorised capital is ú299,900, in 299,900 shares of ú1 ouch; 259,896 shares are issued and fully paid. The capital was originally ú365,750, and 150,000 shares were issued fully paid to shareholders of the old company, and 75,000 issued at 30s. each. In August, 1895, the capital was increased to ú400,000. In January, 1905, the capital was reduced to ú196,500, shareholders receiving one fully-paid share for every two held, and then increased to ú420,000, 196,500 shares being offered to shareholders at par. In August, 1908, the capital wls reduced to ú126,250, shareholders receiving one new share for every four old shares held; it was then increased to ú299,900 and 131,000 shares offered to shareholders at par in the proportion of one new for every three old shares held; the issue was guaranteed. During 1907, ú13,600 six per cent. debentures were issued redeemable at par July 1st, 1912; a further ú2,800 were issued during 1908, redeemable in 1913 and ú17,450 redeemable in 1914; the issues were made at 90 per cent. During 1910, ú72,000 debentures were issued at 90 per cent., ú3,200 at 100 per cent., and in April, 1912, ú6,000 at par, making total debentures now outstanding, ú115,050. Interest is in arrear since July Int, 1911. The accounts for year ended December 31st, 1911, submitted in Johannesburg March 22nd, 1912, showed cash ú290, debtors ú102 ; creditors ú11,799. A share register is kept in London. Share warrants to bearer can be obtained. Transfer form, common; fee, 2s. 6d. per 100 shares or part thereof. Phone: 6336 Central. , Tins company was registered October 10th, 1803, to acquire the freehold of . one-half of the farm Vogelstruisfontein, No. 838, seven miles west of Johannesburg, Witwatersrand, Transvtial, formerly owned by the Vogelstruis Gold Mining Co., Ltd., but purchased by the debenture-holders of that company in 1893 for . ú10,000 cash, and subsequently sold to this company for ú100,000 in fully-paid shares. The property is about 3,200 acres in extent, and includes a mynpacht of 42 outcrop, and 140 deep level main roe claims and various water rights. The township of Hamburg is situate on the estate, and the company receives a propor- : tion of rents paid forstands, and also one-half of the amount paid by other parties for claim licences on its freehold. At June 30th, 1912, 43 claims had been . worked out, leaving 119 claims intact. I, A mill of 80 stamps and cyanide works were started in December, 1896, but i stopped in 1897; the mine has since been developed in the deeper levels, and I crushing was resumed in September, 1899, but, owing to the war, was suspended , l alter nine days work. Crushing was again resumed in March, 1903. A slimes . ,1 plant started work February, 1910. During 1910-11, 101,033 tons yielded 1 ú132,669; costs 22s. 8d. per ton. During 1911-12, 125,128 tons yielded ú148,497 ; costs 201. 10d. per ton. Being a low-grade mine operations were greatly interfered with during 1911-12 owing to scarcity of labour. For crushings see table at commencement of volume. Ore reserves at June, 1911, 147,815 tons; at June 30th, 1912, 120,120 tons. The authorised capital is ú250,000, in 250,000 shares of ú1 each; 224,929 shares are issued and fully paid. The capital was formerly ú200,000; the vendor received 100,000 shares, 50,000 shares were allotted at par, and 49,929 were sold in 1895 at ú3 each. In 1898, 50,000 new shares were created, and in Bep- | tembcr, 1904, 25,010 shares were offered to shareholders at 30:. each, all being 1 taken up. There are ú94,100 six per cent. first mortgage debentures, balance of 1 ú100,000 issued in 1898, redeemable at par on January 1st, 1919. Interest payable January 1st and July 1st. Registered September 19, 1894, in the Transvaal, as the Robinson Deep, Ltd., to acquire from the Goldfields Deep, Ltd., about 148 main reef dip claims south of the Robinson Mine, Witwatersrand, Transvaal. The company was reconstructed and registered in the Transvaal under the present title on July 15, 1899. In January, 1910, 1 03 claims were acquired for ú36,703, and early in 1911, undermining rights covered by an area equal to 5 27 claims were acquired from the Government. The considera tion was ú28,998 per annum for a period of ten years, commencing September, 1911. As from October 1, 1913, ú2,181 per month out of profits is to be placed to a reserve gold account for the purpose of amortisation of the said claims. Phone: 3700 Wall. 1 Registered December 31, 1909, to acquire from Camp Bird, Ltd., an option to├âã ├ ┬ mrchase the controlling interest in the Compania Miners. de Santa Gertrudis y uadolupe, owning the Santa Gertrudis gold and silver mines, for the sum of $9,000,000 Mexican currency say, ú922,131 . The property covers an area of 626 acres, situate about 62 miles from Mexico city, in the mining camp of Pachuca, in the State of Hidalgo, Mexico. The consideration for the option was ú222,869, payable ú20,000 cash and ú202,869 in fully-paid ú1 shares; the vendors also agreed to provide subscribers for 1,072,131 shares at par, and to provide ú150,000 working capital, and also the purchase price of ú922,131, the preliminary expenses to be repaid to the vendors out of the first profits. The property was reported on by R. J. Frecheville, W. J. Cox, E. E. Chase, and F. J. Pope. Two companies have been formed in Mexico, one to operate the mines and the other to treat the ores, all the shares being held by the Santa Gertrudis 00., Ltd. 4k , 1921. 523 SANTA GEBTBUDIS The name of the operating company is Compania de Santa Gertrudis, registered in Mexico January, 1910, with a capital of $250,000. The ore reserves at June 30, 1919, were estimated at 718,579 tons; at June 30, 1920, 476,678 tons, and the recoverable value 24,382 ozs. fine gold and 4,309,556 on. fine silver. The company formed to treat the ores is known as Compania Beneficiadora de Pachuca, registered in Mexico, with $500,000 capital. The plant consists of sixty 1,550 lbs. stamps, ten tube mills, one ball mill, twenty-five agitation tanks, filter presses, &c., with a capacity of 2,000 tons daily. Crushing commenced June 14, 1911. Output For year ended June 30, 1918, 324,762 tons yielded ú836,945. For year ended June 30, 1919, 362,122 tons yielded ú1,037,606 or 57s. 4d. per ton. In June, 1918, the Beneficiadora Co. entered into a working arrangement for a period of twenty years in connection with an important group of mines in the Pachuca district, known as El Bordo, Malinche, El Cristo and La Zorra, having a combined area of 431 acres. This arrangement has since been trans ferred to the Compania General de Inversiones Mineras, S.A., which assumes all responsibility for the fulfilment of the terms, viz., division of the net earnings as to 60 per cent. to the owners of the properties and 40 per cent. to the Beneficiadora 00., with the obligation on the Beneficiadora Co. to provide a sum of $600,000, to be expended on mine development, on equipment, and enlargement of the present Bcneficiadora milling and treatment plant for the purpose of milling and treating the orcs from the leased properties. The Beneficiadora Co. is further under obligation to make an advance to the owners of $500,000, to be repaid out of their 60 per cent. of the first earnings from the properties. An aerial tramway for conveying the ore to the Bene ficiadora mill commenced work October, 1919. At June 30, 1919, the ore reserves of the El Bordo and E Cristo properties were estimated at 569,000 tons and the recoverable value 30,326 ozs. fine gold and 0,582,721 ozs. fine silver In co-operation with Camp Bird, Ltd., the Santa Gertrudis 00., Ltd., has formed the Mexican Corporation, Ltd., a 25 per cent. participation being reserved for Camp Bird, Ltd., and a similar share for Santa Gertrudis, Ltd., and in October, 1919, shareholders in Santa Gertrudis, Ltd., had the right to apply for one Mexican Corporation share at par for every ten Santa Gertrudis shares held. An option has been secured over the Espiritu Santo property of 150 acres in the Real dcl Monte District, and not far from the Santa Gertrudis mine.
